Secure Hydrogen Systems Project

Duration: 2024-Ongoing

Team: University of Birmingham, Multiple Partners

Funding: EPSRC

Project Overview

HI-ACT (Hydrogen Integration for Accelerated Energy Transitions) is a UK-wide research hub uniting academic institutions, industry stakeholders, government agencies, and the public to address key challenges in the integration of hydrogen and alternative liquid fuels (HALF). The hub comprises ten leading UK universities and over forty project partners, fostering interdisciplinary collaboration across research, innovation, and policy domains.

This project contributes to Work Package 3 (Cyber-Physical Industry) and focuses on developing secure, resilient, and efficient architectures for hydrogen integration across industrial and energy sectors.

My Role

As a Research Fellow, my responsibilities include:

  • Developing cybersecurity frameworks for hydrogen infrastructure
  • Analyzing vulnerabilities in cyber-physical systems associated with hydrogen production and distribution
  • Contributing to the design of secure system architectures

Project Objectives

  1. Develop new methodologies for hydrogen system integration
  2. Establish best practices for hydrogen implementation
  3. Create tools for decision-making in hydrogen transitions

Key Outputs

  • Benchmarking Datasets for Hydrogen Energy Systems

  • Cyber Threats to Green Hydrogen Production within a Solar Microgrid
    Authors: Mehmet Bozdal, Zoya Pourmirza
    Publication: International Conference on Security for Information Technology and Communications, 2024
